era: 2008-present. Sludge Rock. E=7 -- slow, massive, psychedelically heavy. Capitol Hill. Sargent House.
associated_musicians: Russian Circles (Sargent House labelmates; Brian Cook Botch/Russian Circles bridge)

genre_lineage.primary: Sludge metal/rock: doom metal + hardcore punk
genre_lineage.new_orleans_origin: Eyehategod (1988, *In the Name of Suffering* 1992), Crowbar, Acid Bath
genre_lineage.pnw_adaptation: Greyer, more psychedelic, more atmospheric than Southern Gothic original
genre_lineage.tempo: Slow (often 60 BPM or below). Riffs are long, repetitive, hypnotic.
genre_lineage.tuning: Downtuned 2-4 steps from standard EADGBE -- typically B or C standard

sargent_house.origin: Management company evolved into record label
sargent_house.aesthetic: Heavy without genre affiliation, atmospheric, melodically sophisticated

discography.label: Sargent House
discography.note: First Sargent House release. National profile begins.

discography.label: Sargent House
discography.note: Title from sound of dripping water. Most sophisticated production.

discography.label: Sargent House
discography.note: Bioluminescent plankton. Pacific Northwest coast imagery.

theory_nodes.concept: Sludge rock lineage: Eyehategod (New Orleans, 1988) created the template -- doom metal slowed to a crawl + hardcore aggression. PNW adapted it: greyer, more atmospheric. Helms Alee inherits both the New Orleans template and the PNW grey.
theory_nodes.description: Sludge: doom + hardcore. New Orleans origin; PNW adaptation.

theory_nodes.concept: Dana James's bass and dual vocals. In sludge, bass is typically support. James's bass is co-equal front-line instrument. Her clean melodic vocals floating above crushing instrumentation creates productive dissonance -- the genre's defining aesthetic device: clean voice against distorted wall.
theory_nodes.description: Dual vocals and bass-as-lead: Helms Alee's distinctive contribution.

theory_nodes.concept: Sargent House as heavy art music independent. Founded 1998 Cathy Pellow. Chelsea Wolfe, Russian Circles, Deafheaven, Emma Ruth Rundle. Brian Cook (Russian Circles) previously in Botch (254) -- label bridge to next degree.
theory_nodes.description: Sargent House roster and Brian Cook bridge.

cross_references.relationship: Noise rock to sludge. Underground to underground. E=7 holds. Speed changes: noise rock fast; sludge slow. Both self-sufficient, outside institutional music.
E rises to 8. Sludge to post-hardcore/mathcore. Capitol Hill to Tacoma. Brian Cook bridge: Botch → Russian Circles → Sargent House → Helms Alee.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 vs E=2. Both are low-frequency, both move at geological/hydraulic speed. Sludge moves sonic energy slowly; tidal current moves billions of cubic meters slowly. Mass against mass.

milestones: SLUDGE ROCK ENTERS ENGINE (first sludge entry)
